{{Short description="Elementary particle; fundamental constituent of matter}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Quark
| image = Quark structure proton.svg
| caption = The [[proton]] consists of two [[up quark]]s and one [[down quark]] held together by the [[strong interaction]].
| topics = [[Elementary particle]], [[Strong interaction]], [[Proton]], [[Neutron]], [[Gluon]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Fermion]]
| types = up, down, charm, strange, top, bottom
| charge = +2/3 e (u, c, t); −1/3 e (d, s, b)
| spin = 1/2
}}

A '''quark''' is a type of [[elementary particle]] and a fundamental constituent of [[matter]]. Quarks combine to form [[composite particle]]s called [[hadron]]s, the most stable of which are [[proton]]s and [[neutron]]s, the components of [[atomic nucleus|atomic nuclei]]. Quarks are the only elementary particles in the [[Standard Model]] to experience all four [[fundamental interaction]]s.

== Properties ==

=== Flavours ===
There are six types ("flavours") of quarks, grouped into three generations:
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Generation !! Quark !! Charge !! Mass
|-
| 1st || [[Up quark]] (u) || +2/3 e || ~2.2 MeV/c²
|-
| 1st || [[Down quark]] (d) || −1/3 e || ~4.7 MeV/c²
|-
| 2nd || [[Charm quark]] (c) || +2/3 e || ~1.28 GeV/c²
|-
| 2nd || [[Strange quark]] (s) || −1/3 e || ~96 MeV/c²
|-
| 3rd || [[Top quark]] (t) || +2/3 e || ~173 GeV/c²
|-
| 3rd || [[Bottom quark]] (b) || −1/3 e || ~4.18 GeV/c²
|}

=== Colour charge ===
Quarks carry a [[colour charge]] (red, green, or blue), which is the source of the [[strong interaction]]. Gluons mediate the force between quarks.

=== Confinement ===
Quarks have never been observed in isolation. Due to [[colour confinement]], they are always bound within [[hadron]]s. Attempting to separate quarks creates new quark–antiquark pairs from the vacuum.

== Hadrons ==
=== Baryons ===
Three-quark combinations:
* [[Proton]] (<math>uud</math>) — stable
* [[Neutron]] (<math>udd</math>) — stable in nuclei; free neutron decays via beta decay

=== Mesons ===
Quark–antiquark pairs (<math>q\bar{q}</math>):
* [[Pion]]s (<math>\pi^+, \pi^0, \pi^-</math>) — mediators of the nuclear force

== Asymptotic freedom ==
At extremely short distances (high energies), the strong interaction between quarks becomes weak — a property called [[asymptotic freedom]], discovered by [[David Gross]], [[Frank Wilczek]], and [[David Politzer]] ([[Nobel Prize in Physics|Nobel Prize]] 2004).

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Down quark]]
* [[Elementary particle]]
* [[Gluon]]
* [[Hadron]]
* [[Proton]]
* [[Strong interaction]]
* [[Up quark]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Quarks| ]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Particle physics]]
